Tests for the `toFixed` method on numbers, including edge cases and error handling.

# Purpose
This code is a test suite for the JavaScript `Number.prototype.toFixed` method, which formats a number using fixed-point notation. The tests verify the method's behavior with various inputs, including positive and negative numbers, very small and very large numbers, and special values like `Infinity` and `NaN`. The assertions check if the output of `toFixed` matches the expected string representation for different decimal places. The code also includes tests for error handling, ensuring that `toFixed` throws a `TypeError` when called on a non-number object and a `RangeError` when the specified number of decimal places is out of the valid range. The comment notes that the test will not pass on systems using `FLOAT32` due to precision issues.
